content:
Another example of symbolism Welty implements within the story is her decision to use the name ‘Phoenix,’ as the characters’ first name. A phoenix is a bird from mythology that symbolizes themes of renewal and hope based on the fact that in the myth, the phoenix is described as setting itself on fire, being consumed by the flames, and eventually is born again to rise from the ashes. This form of symbolism correlates with the theme of the story such as perseverance over hardship because although Jackson intuitively knows she is not young enough anymore to face such strenuous physical challenges such as venturing through the woods; she chooses to do it anyway. In this way, Jackson makes the decision to symbolically be reborn as the phoenix in order to assist her ill grandson, showcasing her commitment and resolve to rise above her present challenge.

The story is powerful due to the fact that Welty utilizes themes that are relatable in common everyday life. For example, performing a selfless act to help a loved one is common in healthy relationships in everyday life. Furthermore, having to endure difficulties when attempting to accomplish something significant is also a common occurrence in everyday life. In total, Welty narrates an engaging story about Miss Jackson, who ultimately proves to be a showcase of a character that chooses to rise above and beyond limitations.
